channel.title: Globehint
channel.description: Deep, honest travel guides for every destination on earth.

item.title: Düsseldorf Travel Guide
item.description: Discover Germany's fashion capital, where futuristic harbor design meets a historic square dubbed the longest bar in the world.

item.title: Bad Ischl Travel Guide
item.description: Trade the Alpine crowds of Hallstatt for the grand imperial spa town of Bad Ischl, famous for its thermal waters, royal palace, and world-class pastries.

item.title: Bad Zwischenahn Travel Guide
item.description: Escape the city for a peaceful day at the Ammerland region's premier lake resort, packed with lush gardens, smoked delicacies, and classic thatched architecture.

item.title: Bremen Travel Guide
item.description: Forget Berlin's grit or Munich's polish. Bremen pairs medieval fairy-tale quarters with a rowdy, maritime grit and an obsession with local beer.

item.title: Bremerhaven Travel Guide
item.description: Germany's rugged North Sea gateway ditches fairytale aesthetics for industrial weight, world-class modern museums, and some of the best raw seafood rolls you will ever eat.

item.title: Cuxhaven Travel Guide
item.description: Escape to where the Elbe meets the North Sea for windy beaches, historical lighthouses, and a unique walk across the ocean floor.

item.title: Dachstein Krippenstein Travel Guide
item.description: Escape the crowded lakeside streets of Hallstatt for the jaw-dropping dramatic heights of the Dachstein Alps, complete with subterranean ice worlds and a thrilling walk over a 400-meter drop.

item.title: Gosausee Alpine Lakes Travel Guide
item.description: A dramatic trio of glacial lakes framed by the jagged peaks of the Dachstein Alps, offering the perfect outdoor escape just outside Hallstatt.

item.title: Hamburg Travel Guide
item.description: Germany's fierce, water-obsessed northern hub where grit meets high culture along legendary canals and harbor docks.

item.title: Ludwigslust Palace Travel Guide
item.description: Swap Schwerin for the 'Versailles of the North' to wander through opulent paper-mache ballrooms and a sprawling 127-hectare forest park.

item.title: Lübeck Travel Guide
item.description: A striking medieval island city wrapped by the Trave River, famous as the historic capital of the Hanseatic League.

item.title: Lüneburg Travel Guide
item.description: A gorgeous medieval salt-mining town of brick Gothic facades, crooked half-timbered lanes, and water-side beer gardens that escaped WWII entirely unscathed.

item.title: Oldenburg Travel Guide
item.description: A handsomely understated ducal city submerged in greenery, where bikes outnumber cars and North German hospitality rules the historic cobblestones.

item.title: Poel Island Travel Guide
item.description: Escape the city for wide sandy beaches, a historic 13th-century church, fresh fish buns, and the tranquil pace of Baltic island life.

item.title: Schwerin Travel Guide
item.description: A stunning lakeside capital dominated by a fairytale island palace, offering rich history and a beautifully quiet northern German escape.

item.title: Travemünde Travel Guide
item.description: Trade the brick Gothic spires of Lübeck for the breezy Baltic coast, historic sailing ships, and the ultimate seaside street food.

item.title: Wismar Travel Guide
item.description: A UNESCO-listed Hanseatic gem on Germany's Baltic coast famed for monumental Brick Gothic churches, a historic old harbor, and fresh fish buns straight off the boats.

item.title: Worpswede Travel Guide
item.description: Escape into the peaceful moorlands and explore the historic red-brick expressionist architecture and studios of Germany's most famous artists' colony.

item.title: Why Travelling Properly Matters
item.description: True travel isn't about ticking boxes. It is about slowing down, ditching the generic top-ten lists, and leaning into the local context that makes a place real.

item.title: Berchtesgaden & Eagle's Nest Travel Guide
item.description: Scale the Bavarian Alps to explore Adolf Hitler's former mountain retreat, stand among jagged alpine peaks, and uncover the dark history of the Obersalzberg complex.
